codeReview.summary: This re-review resolves three of the four round-1 blockers. XcmInbox is now AccessManaged and covered by unauthorized-caller tests, event-correlator now prefers closest-block matches with regression coverage, and the README market-creation section now points at deployMarketVersion, uses migrateBorrower(fromVersionId, toVersionId), and documents coordinator permission wiring. However, the liquidation-hook-registry blocker is not resolved: commit deda919 does not change LiquidationHookRegistry, whose storage/execution model still supports only one handler per hook type. Because the original feature and VAL-ASYNC-002 require sequential execution of all registered hooks, the milestone should still be considered failed after this fix feature.

issues.description: The original blocking issue remains. The registry still stores a single handler per hook type (`mapping(bytes32 => address)`) and `executeHooks` still loads and invokes only that one address (`/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/contracts/LiquidationHookRegistry.sol:31-32,107-114`). Commit `deda919` does not modify this file or its tests, so the new rationale in `scrutiny-fixes-m10` does not satisfy the original `liquidation-hook-registry` acceptance criteria (`executeHooks calls all registered hooks`) or `VAL-ASYNC-002` (`hooks are called in sequence during liquidation`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared state now contains conflicting hook-registry requirements. The original feature and validation contract require sequential/all-hook execution, but the fix feature description redefines the requirement as satisfied by a single registered handler. That contradiction should be reconciled so future workers are not guided by competing specs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/features.json:1837-1846` says `executeHooks` iterates registered hooks and `expectedBehavior` is `executeHooks calls all registered hooks`; `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/validation-contract.md:471-473` says hooks are called in sequence; `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/features.json:1985-1993` says single-handler-per-type is intentional and sufficient.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `solidity-worker` still requires `npx hardhat compile` in the full validation suite, but this fix handoff omitted compile and still marked `followedProcedure: true`. Either the skill should explicitly allow skipping compile when other validators already compile touched contracts, or handoffs should record that omission as a justified deviation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/skills/solidity-worker/SKILL.md:47-50` lists `npm test`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, and `npx hardhat compile`; `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/handoffs/2026-03-19T01-19-00-337Z__scrutiny-fixes-m10__d4fc208e-c9c8-4456-8678-ef5d45676309.json:16-38,79-81` records only focused tests, `npm test`, lint, and typecheck while `followedProcedure` stays true.

summary: Re-reviewed commit deda919 and the fix-session transcript. XcmInbox, event-correlator, and market-factory-docs blockers are resolved, but liquidation-hook-registry is unchanged and still fails the original sequential/all-hooks requirement, so milestone permissionless-async-bilateral should still be considered failed after this fix.
